title: penstemon humilis var. brevifolius
intro: Penstemon humilis var. brevifolius, also known as the small penstemon or dwarf beardtongue, is a low-growing, perennial plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Penstemon humilis var. brevifolius include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
